reqCond.para: Aircraft jacked and supported on maintenance stands (ATA 07).
Hydraulic system pressurized at 3000 PSI ±100 PSI (ATA 29).
Gear warning circuit armed and cockpit gear handle in DOWN position.

reqPerson.para: Two licensed aircraft maintenance engineers (AMEs) required: one in cockpit, one observing gear bays.

supply.para: Hydraulic fluid Mil-PRF-5606 (for top-up if required)

warning.warningAndCautionPara: WARNING: Keep clear of all gear bays and door travel paths during retraction and extension cycles. Inadvertent gear movement can cause serious injury or death.
WARNING: Aircraft must be securely supported on jacks before performing any gear retraction test. Loss of aircraft support can result in structural damage and personal injury.

caution.warningAndCautionPara: CAUTION: Do not exceed three (3) retraction/extension cycles without allowing a 5-minute hydraulic system cooling period.
CAUTION: Verify hydraulic fluid level before and after the test. Do not operate the hydraulic system below minimum fluid level.

proceduralStep.para: Pre-check actions:
Verify aircraft is level on jacks. Check jack pad pressures are nominal.
Confirm hydraulic system pressure reads 3000 PSI ±100 PSI on cockpit hydraulic pressure indicator.
Confirm gear position indicator shows three GREEN lights (gear down and locked).
Confirm second AME is clear of all gear bays and door travel paths. Establish voice communication between cockpit and bay observer.
Retraction cycle — Cycle 1 of 3:
Cockpit AME: Place gear handle in UP position.
Start timer simultaneously with gear handle movement to UP.
Observe: all three RED lights illuminate (gear in transit).
Bay observer: confirm inboard gear doors open before gear leg movement begins. Report any abnormal noise or binding to cockpit AME.
Stop timer when cockpit gear position indicator shows lights OFF (gear up and locked).
Record retraction time. Acceptance criterion: retraction time ≤ 8 seconds. If retraction time exceeds 8 seconds, stop test and refer to fault isolation procedure in AMM 32-30-00.
Extension cycle — Cycle 1 of 3:
Cockpit AME: Place gear handle in DOWN position.
Start timer simultaneously with gear handle movement to DOWN.
Observe: all three RED lights illuminate (gear in transit).
Stop timer when cockpit gear position indicator shows three GREEN lights (gear down and locked).
Record extension time. Acceptance criterion: extension time ≤ 10 seconds (normal hydraulic power). If extension time exceeds 10 seconds, stop test and refer to AMM 32-30-00 fault isolation.
Bay observer: confirm outboard gear doors are fully closed with gear in the down-and-locked position. Report any gap or misalignment.

Repeat STEP-RETRACT-CYCLE and STEP-EXTEND-CYCLE two more times (Cycles 2 and 3) to complete the full three-cycle test. Record all retraction and extension times. All six recorded times must meet acceptance criteria before proceeding.
Emergency free-fall extension check (if required by maintenance program):
Ensure gear is in the UP and locked position.
Deactivate hydraulic power to the landing gear retraction circuit per AMM 29-10-00.
Operate emergency gear extension handle. Start timer.
Stop timer when three GREEN lights illuminate.
Record emergency extension time. Acceptance criterion: ≤ 15 seconds. Verify nitrogen backup pressure per AMM 32-60-00 if time is marginal.
Post-test actions:
Confirm gear position indicator shows three GREEN lights (gear down and locked) at end of test.
Check hydraulic fluid level. Top up with Mil-PRF-5606 if required. Record quantity added.
Inspect all hydraulic lines and fittings for leakage. No leakage is permitted. If leakage found, isolate and rectify before returning aircraft to service.
Make maintenance record entry: retraction/extension times recorded, acceptance criteria met (or deferred rectification reference if not met), date, AME license number, signature.

reqCond.para: Aircraft lowered from jacks per AMM 07-10-00 only after confirming: gear down and locked (three GREEN), hydraulic pressure nominal, no fluid leaks found.
